div1.entryFree: Tiny (O. Edd. tine or tyne), small, puny (always joined with little, by way of giving the expression some tenderness): when that I was and a t. little boy, Tw. V, 398. any pretty little t. kickshaws, H4B V, 1, 29. my little t. thief, V, 3, 60. he that has and a little t. wit, Lr. III, 2, 74.
